title: chroot: use user namespaces only when necessary, check if they're enabled on error
body:
When running with chroot isolation, only create a new user namespace when we have mappings to set.

If we get an error when trying to create a new user namespace, check `/proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces` to verify that user namespaces are even available.

comments.body: may also be the resolution to https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1058 ?

I think so, at least the bits where we run afoul of not being able to create user namespaces even in cases where we don't need to do any ID mapping.  The unit test that's failing in CI appears to be only reproducible when running under podman, still digging.
@nalind looks like Red Hat CI isn't happy and it doesn't appear to be a flake.
